quickInsight: General Conference is like getting a personal text from God — but only if you're actually paying attention when it comes through.
keyThemes: Listening for personal revelation during Conference
Sustaining and following living prophets and apostles
Finding answers to your own questions through Conference talks
Acting on what you hear — not just hearing it
The courage to stand as a witness even when it's not popular
discussionQuestions: If you could sit down with any General Authority and ask one question about your life right now, what would it be — and did any talk this weekend come close to answering it?
Why do you think God uses regular people (prophets, apostles) to deliver His messages instead of just speaking to everyone directly?
What's the difference between hearing a Conference talk and actually letting it change something about your week?
Have you ever had a moment during Conference where something hit different — like it was meant specifically for you? What was that like?
How do you decide which voices to trust — whether it's a prophet at the pulpit, a friend at school, or someone on social media?
teachingIdeas: Before class, have each young man write down one real question or challenge they're dealing with right now — fold it up, keep it private. After discussing Conference themes, ask them to look at their paper again and share (if comfortable) whether anything from Conference spoke to that question. This makes it personal without forcing vulnerability.

Play 30-second clips from 2-3 Conference talks and have the quorum rate them 'skip, save, or share' like they would a song. Then discuss why certain messages hit harder than others and what that says about how the Spirit communicates with them individually.

Use the analogy of a coach's halftime speech: General Conference is God's halftime talk to us. The game is still going. The coach isn't yelling — he's adjusting the game plan, reminding you what you're capable of, and sending you back out. Ask: what adjustments did the prophet ask us to make this week?

Have each young man pick ONE sentence from any Conference talk that stuck with them. Write it on a sticky note and put it somewhere they'll see it every day this week — bathroom mirror, phone case, locker. Follow up at mutual to see if it made a difference.
activities: Conference Challenge Night: At mutual, set up stations where pairs of young men get a Conference talk summary and have 3 minutes to create a 60-second TikTok-style explanation of the talk's main point for their age group. Vote on the best one. Reinforces actually processing what was taught — not just sitting through it.

Prophet Scavenger Hunt: Give teams a list of specific principles or promises from this Conference and have them race to find the exact talk, speaker, and quote. First team to match them all wins. Builds familiarity with Conference content in a competitive way they'll actually enjoy.

Goal-Setting Fireside: Around a fire pit or in a casual setting, have each young man share one thing from Conference they want to apply before next General Conference in October. Write them on cards, seal them in envelopes, and commit to opening them together in six months to see how they did.
